title: The One With Phoebe's Birthday Dinner

lines.text: Central Perk. All except Chandler, plus Emma.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh hey you guys, I couldn't get a reservation for the night of my birthday, so we have to do dinner Thursday night instead.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thursday? But that's Halloween.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: [It's just] So spooky, that's all.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So, so, is Mike coming to dinner?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No! It's my first birthday with a boyfriend, and he has to work. Uch, I get mad at him, but I think it's a little to soon to show my true colors.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Pheebs, I would make a reservation for five, because one of us has to stay home and watch Emma. (to Ross:) Which one of us should go to dinner?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, Rachel!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Actually, um, I was thinking maybe both of us could go.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, yay!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thanks, I put a lot of extra thoughts on your gift.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Alright, okay, so we can all go now. That is fun. Hey, you know what? We all haven't been together the six of us in such a long time.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are you talking about? We're all together right now.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Um, Mon, Chandler's not here.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, dear god!
Opening Credits
Tulsa, a conference room. Chandler is chairing a group of eight people.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Good morning everyone, it's nice to see our team together for the first time. Now, before we get started, are there any questions? (colleague raises hand) Yes, Ken is it?

lines.speaker: Ken
lines.text: That's right. Is it true, that the reason you are here in Tulsa is that you fell asleep in a meeting and took the job without realizing what you were saying yes to?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (laughs) Well, don't believe everything you hear, Ken. (both turning away to files) But yeah, that's true. Alright, let's get started, by take a look at last quarter's figures. (The female next to Chandler starts smoking, towards her:) Ah, Claudia, aren't you supposed to blow smoke up the bosses' ass?

lines.speaker: Claudia
lines.text: I'm sorry. Does the smoke bother you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, no, no-no-no. I smoked for years, then I quit. Right now, I can't remember why. (to everyone:) You're not allowed to smoke in this office. Not right?

lines.speaker: Claudia
lines.text: Yes, in Oklahoma it's legal to smoke in offices with fifteen people or less. (passing the pack) Would you like one?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Alright, lo...look. I don't smoke anymore. But if the rest of you want to light up, go ahead, it's fine. (everyone lights up) So you all smoke then? That's almost rude, that I'm not.

lines.speaker: Ken
lines.text: That's not true. If you don't wanna smoke ...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (loud) Ken, please! No, I can't, I can't smoke. If I smoke, my wife would kill me.

lines.speaker: Ken
lines.text: I'm sorry, but isn't your wife back in New York?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I always liked you, Ken. (takes a cigarette)
The Bings'. Monica in bathrobe, merely covered. Someone's knocking at the door.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (from outside) Trick or treat! (It's obviously Halloween eve, the night of her birthday dinner.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (opening the door) Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, and treat it is. (Monica's breasts.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hmhmm. (ties up)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow, so glad I changed. Almost wore my ??? outfit that can't contain my breasts.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This is not, what I'm wearing. I'm ovulating and Chandler's gonna be home any minute, so I thought we would try before dinner.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohh. Oh wait! (Jis) you guys won't be late for my dinner, will you?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Believe me, Chandler and I have not seen each other in over a week. We'll probably be the first ones there.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: 'kay, see you there. Happy humping! (outside meeting Chandler.) Hey...hey! Oh, wow, somebody smoked out here? Oh my god, don't people know, you're not allowed to smoke in public spaces?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Actually, in Oklahoma smoking is legal in all commune areas and offices with fewer than fifteen people.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You smoked!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No! I just happened t'do a lot trivias about smoking in different states. For example, in Hawaii cigarettes are called Leyhallalookoos.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (smells at him) Chandler, you stink of cigarettes.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uch, do you think, Monica is gonna be able smell it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Are you kidding? The woman has the nose of a bloodhound ... and the breasts of a Greek goddess.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Pheebs?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (embarrassed) I'm gonna go. (leaves to stairs)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (getting in) Okay, something to cover the smell ... Oven cleaner! (sprays himself, reads label) Unscented!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (naughty in doorway) Welcome home. I've missed you. join me in the bedroom?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No thanks, I'm good.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (comes over) O-kay, so you wanna play it that way, do you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (shrinks back) Right. You know what? Actually I just get off the plane, so I'm feeling kinda gross. Maybe I should just take a shower.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You don't need a shower.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (still backs away) Alright, the truth is, I soiled myself during some turbulences.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What do I smell? (sniffs him) I smell smoke. Huh-did you smoke?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, but I just had one. Two. Two tiny cigarettes. Okay, five. A pack. Two pack...a...a carton. Three big fat cartons in two days. (How many cigarettes are there in one pack and how many packs in one carton in the US?) But it's over, I made a decision, I'm not gonna smoke anymore.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (gets a pack out of his jacket)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But, those are for you.
Ross and Rachel's. Ross phones, Rachel and Emma are there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Alright, we'll just, uh, see when you get here. Bye. (hangs up) Huh, that was my mom, she's stuck in terrible traffic.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, well that's now the third sign that I should not leave Emma.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, what were the other two?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, let's see. The first one is: I don't want to. And, you know, I'm not going.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know, it's the first time, we're leaving the baby and ... hey, I know how hard it is for you, but ... but Emma is gonna be fine. My mom is gonna be with her. She's great with kids.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: She is?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ya.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What about (?) Monica.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, you only heard Monica's side of that. That little fatso was a terror.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ish. I just don't think I can bear it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rachel, I know that you can. And you should.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uch.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Really, it would be good for you and in fact, why don't you, why don't you go ahead to the restaurant and I will wait for my mom and then I'll meet you there.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh-A.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, no, really. You should go. Just go! Go! Go out! Really, the world is your oyster. Kick up the heels. Paint the town red. (Slang right?)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You need to learn some new slang.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm serious. C'mon, you should go. Here. (shoves her outside, while she tries to stay) No, uh-uh, just go.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What ... Oh! (points inside)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! No, you know what? (closes the door) You [can't get in there] (?), the baby's fine, now squam (?). Yeah, [I told you a|Tell your] story walking. (?)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I was just going to say that I left my keys.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, (door is locked) holy molly are we in a pickle now.
The restaurant. Phoebe and Joey are sitting alone at a table for six.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Where is everyone? They're forty minutes late.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know, u-uch.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm starving. I know we were coming here tonight, I ate nothing all day.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What about me, he? Only had one lunch today.

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: (with British accent) Soo, are we expecting the rest of our party shortly?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes, they are expected presently. Yeah, yeah um, their arrival is in the offing.

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Right. We do have a table for two available, perhaps you would be more comfortable.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, they're comin', we're waitin' right here.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Joseph! (to waiter) Thou needn't worry, they shan't be long.

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: It's just that we do have some large parties waiting.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: One really does have a stick up one's ass. Doesn't one?
The Bings'.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How can you smoke in this day of age? Do you not seen that ad with a little kid walks to grandpa, it's chilling.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I messed up, it was a meeting, everybody was smoking.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So what? Don't you have any will power?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Will power? I've watch home movies of you eating ding-dongs (?) without taking the tin foil off.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You said that was sexy!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: 'kay, look: Can we just drop this? I'm not gonna smoke again.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's right, because I forbid you to smoke again.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You forbid me?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Mhmm.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know, I flew a long way t see my loving wife? Is she here by the way?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Don't joke (?) with me, okay? I'm very, very upset right now.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, would you say this was the most upset you could be?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Then, I might as well ... (grabs the cigarettes) do this (lights one, exhales). Not really sure what to do now.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, I'll tell you what we're gonna do: We are already late for Phoebe's birthday dinner, so you point out put out that cigarette, we're gonna put this fight on hold and go have sex.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Fine. What!?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Sex! This is the last day I'm ovulating, and when we don't do it now, we'll have to wait till next month. (walks towards bedroom)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You serious? (follows)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh yeah!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Right, fine, I'll do it, but no talking.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Huh, and no cuddling.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And no kissing your neck.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh good, I hate it when you do that

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And lots of kissing your neck.
Outside Ross and Rachel's.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, well the ??? is not home.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No. Uch.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh-oh, wait, my mother is gonna be here any minute. And she has the keys.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Alright, I can't, I can't wait that long. You have to do something-knock that door down!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I would, but I bruise like a peach. Besides, y...you know, everything is gonna be fine. The baby's sleeping.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What if she jumped out the bassinet?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Can't hold her own head up, but yeah jumped.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my god, I left the water running.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rach you did not leave the water running. Please, just pull yourself together, okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ah, did I leave the stove on?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You never cooked since 1996. (Actually Rachel cooked in "609 - TOW Ross Got High", first aired 1999-11-25.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Is the window open? Because if there's a window open, a bird could fly in there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my god, you know what, yeah, I think you're right. I think ... listen, listen!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ubb.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: A pigeon, a pigeon. (previously scared Rachel turns away) No, no wait, no-no, an eagle flew in. Landed on the stove and caught fire. The baby, seeing this, jumps across the apartment to the mighty bird's aid. The eagle, however, misconstrues as an act of aggression and grabs the baby on its talon. Meanwhile the faucet fills the apartment with water. Baby and bird still up lays (?) are locked in a death grip, swirling around the whirl pool, that fills the apartment.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Boy, are you gonna be sorry if that's true.
The restaurant with still just Phoebe and Joey.

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Phoebe & Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: It's been an hour. ??? be willing to reconsider switching to a smaller table.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Maybe we should just eat now.

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: You can't order until your entire party has arrived. Restaurant policy.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wha-a how about this: Another table leaves, right? But there's still some food left on their place, okay, what's the restaurant's policy about people eatin' that?

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Estrangement (?).

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But it happens? (waiter leaves) I'm gotta go to the bathroom.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, you can't go. No-no-no, I can't hold this table on my own. If they ask me to move, I cave.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: If you ask me to stay, I'll pee. (leaves)

lines.speaker: Maitre D'
lines.text: Good evening, Miss. (Phoebe turns her head away) Miss? (from the opposite side) Miss? (she turns again) Miss!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, fine, I'll move. Alright, you don't have to manhandle me. (gets up) Where? (he points at a smaller table) Okay. Thank you. Wach.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (returns to their old table where elderly people are sitting now, sits) Finally you guys made it. (looks up, turns left to Phoebe's chair) Pheebs, who the hell-uhuhh!
The Bings' bedroom, Chandler is undressing, Monica in bed already.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Spend more time with the tie. That'll make a baby.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, I can't do this. I can't make luv to you while we're fighting this way.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh sure, now you're Mister Sensitivity. But when you wanted to have sex right after my uncle's funeral

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That was a celebration of life. Alright, look, I'm not gonna do this. Alright, is this really the way you want a baby to be conceived?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, you're right. Mnya, we shouldn't do it like this. Huch. For what it's worth, I'm, I'm sorry. I shouldn't have come down on you so hard about the smoking. So you had a few cigarettes, not the end of the world.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Mean it?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You are incredible. Unless, I...I'm not gonna smoke again. And if I do, I promise, I will hide it so much better from you. (they kiss)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: D'you want to?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, let's celebrate life!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: 'kay.
Ms. Geller, Rachel and Ross storm into the apartment.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Och, god. (seeing Emma) Oh, thank god, you're okay. I'm so sorry we left you. Mom never gonna leave you again. Never ever ever again. Uch.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Great. So let's get going?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh no. I mean it. After what just happened, I'm never leaving her again.

lines.speaker: Ms. Geller
lines.text: I understand, separation is hard. One time I was about to leave Ross to go to the beauty parlor and he got so upset, he took off all his clothes, tucked his ??? between his legs and cried out: "Mommy, I'm a girl, take me with you."

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Somehow over time it got easier to be apart from you.
Bings in bed, finished.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uhh. You are welcome.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You know what? Let's not talk.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uch. I am still so mad at you for smoking.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But you said you forgave me. It was just a couple of cigarettes-no big deal.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, blablablabaybaybay.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Leave it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I was just saying that because I was ovulating and you said you wouldn't have sex with me while we're fighting.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You tricked me to get me into bed?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's right, I got mine.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I feel so used.
Restaurant, still just the not-couple.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I guess they're not coming. You wanna just order?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thank you. (stands up and kisses her lips.) Waiter! 'lright, this is gonna be fast, so try to keep up: Risotto with the shaped truffles and the roasted rip steak with the golden Chanterelles and a Bordelaise sauce and that any that stuff I just said means snails. (Hope, *I* kept up.)

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Er-does not.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Tomato tart and which of the pastas would you recommend?

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Oh, they're both exclus...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Both it is, thank you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, uh, again. Can I make a special request: Can you bring everything as soon as it's ready? Appetizers, entrees, we don't care.

lines.speaker: Ross & Rachel
lines.text: (entering) Hey, hi, hi!

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: I'll just wait to put your order in.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You guys are over an hour late. What happened to you two?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm so sorry ...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: We got locked out of the apartment, we ...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's a great story-can I eat it?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And then Rachel wasn't sure she could leave the baby.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: N-it wasn't easy, but it's your birthday and I did what I got to do.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And that's Judy over there at the bar with Emma?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh honey, this is for the best, thus I'm not distracted, worrying about Emma, how she's doing at home and I'm being completely here with you and, oh, she spit up!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: She spit up. Judy! She spi...Judy! Look alive, Judy! (they sit down) Thank you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thanks. Oh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, ooh, everything looks delicious. What should I ha-ave? What should I have?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (mumbling) Never hit a woman. Never hit a woman.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y'know this ??? is incredible.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ross bruises like a peach. He bruises like a peach.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, I'll have the fixed salad and the duck.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yah, I'll have the soup and the salmon.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And remember whatever comes up first. Okay? And hurry, because ...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering with husband) Happy birthday!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Son of a bitch!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wher-where have you been?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, we had a little fight.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I would never lie to get someone into bed.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You used to tell girls you were a Kennedy. (being sat down) Ooh, uh, thanks. Wow, little tight, isn't it? How d'you get a bigger table? You-you had a big table, but they made you move. Huh-huh, shut up Monica. Whoo, I suppose that Chandler will have the smoked duck.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I suppose that Monica will have the ... manipulative shrew.

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: I'll give you another minute.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why are you going? He said, she wanted the shrew! (runs after the waiter)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rach, c'mon, Emma is fine. You're turning into an obsessive mother. Okay, you need to stop.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y'guys ever heard the story about when Rosses mom went to the beauty salon?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You mean the lully story?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (childish) Huh-huh, they already know it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You guys, we've been waiting for you for a long time, maybe you should order.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (returned) No, no, it's okay, I already told the waiter what they want.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why would you do that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Chandler, control your woman!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (lifts for a toast) Okay, as everybody has ordered, I would like to start the celebration and make a toast ... to Phoebe. She dropped her sock.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Aw ... what?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: N-no, Emma dropped her sock.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Mom's here? I wanted to have lunch with her today, she told me she was out of town.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, she still has not noticed that the baby's sock is on the ground.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: 's a good toast.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to Ross) Could you please get her attention?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: W-oa ... Mommy! (gestures to his not understanding mother)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (getting up) Oh, for god's sake, Judy, pick up the sock! Pick up the sock! Pick up the sock! (everybody scared) I'm sorry, was that rude? Di-did my, my li-little outburst blunt (?) the hideousness (?) that is this evening? Look, I know, you all have a lot going on, but all I wanted to do was have dinner with my friends on my birthday. And you are all so late and you didn't even have the courtesy to call. (her cellular rings) Well, it's too late now.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, ??? think that's us?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: well, this is, this is, this is not over! (on phone) Hello?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to Chandler and Monica) Well, what is going on with you two?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uch, you see, I'm ovulating.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah, that's what she says. But maybe you're not ovulating at all, maybe it's just a clever ruse to get me into bed.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes smokie, that is what it was. I just can't get enough.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Joey) You not gonna believe this: She lied! She tricked me into having sex with her.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So? Did have sex, right?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What's the matter with me? Why I'm such a girl?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (returns) Okay, that was Mike.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe, hi, we're so sorry. You're totally right. We are here one hundred per cent and we love you and we are ready to start your birthday celebration.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Mhuh, guys, that means the world to me. Huh, nkay, I'm gonna take off.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I love you guys too, but Mike got off work early. Wait. Wait, I'm not t-not that kind of girl that just ditches her friends to be with her boyfriend. You know what? I am. Bye guys! (waves to the bar) Judy! Bye.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh thank god, if Phoebe's going, can we please take Emma home?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You know, I think that's a good idea-our babysitter just pounded in another Chardonnet. (both get up) Bye, y'guys.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Bye.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: See ya. Well, this is just us.

lines.speaker: Monica & Chandler
lines.text: Mhum.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So, I'm, I'm probably still ovulating. Do you want to give it another try?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So you never had sex with a Kennedy, have you?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Do, do you gonna do it now?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We don't have much time. Once the egg descended the oviduct ...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No-ohoh. (the Bings leave, the waiter comes)

lines.speaker: Maitre D'
lines.text: I sincerely hope the rest of your party is returning.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nah, just me. All alone. (all the food is served) Dinner for six for one, uh, you boys are about to see something really special.
Closing Credits
continues, Joey finished everything.

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: How was everything, sir?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Excellent. The shrew in particular was exclusive.

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Well, I hope, you got some room left.

lines.speaker: Waiters
lines.text: (with birthday cake, singing) Happy birthday to you, happy birthday to you, happy birthday dear ...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Joey! Joey.

lines.speaker: Waiters
lines.text: ... Joey, happy birthday to you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's the best birthday ever.
End
